content.content:
1â†’# Transflow Roadmap (MVP â†’ Iteration)
     2â†’
     3â†’Goal: Orchestrate automated code transformation flows reusing the platformâ€™s existing building blocks (ARF, orchestration, unified storage/config), delivered in thin, incremental phases that can ship and run ASAP.
     4â†’
     5â†’Streams are designed to be largely independent, minimizing interference and enabling parallel delivery. Each stream is split into phases under `roadmap/transflow/stream-*/phase-*.md` with clear acceptance criteria and TDD guidance.
     6â†’
     7â†’## Streams Overview
     8â†’
     9â†’- Stream 1: MVP Transflow (Implemented + In Progress)
    10â†’  - Phase 1 âś… (Implemented): OpenRewrite recipe â†’ Build check (no deploy) via `/v1/apps/:app/builds`
    11â†’  - Phase 2 âš ď¸Ź (In Progress): LangGraph planner/reducer as jobs for buildâ€‘error healing with parallel options (human, LLMâ€‘exec, ORWâ€‘generated)
    12â†’
    13â†’- Stream 2: LLM Execution + Planning
    14â†’  - Phase 1 âš ď¸Ź (Partial): LLMâ€‘Exec (sequential) as Nomad jobs, MCP context, model registry under `llms` (used by Stream 1 Phase 2 branches)
    15â†’  - Phase 2: LLMâ€‘Plan (LangGraph) to emit exec steps; optional beyond MVP since planner/reducer exist in Stream 1
    16â†’
    17â†’- Stream 3: Merge Request to GitLab
    18â†’  - Phase 1: Create/update MR on GitLab (project from `target_repo`, target `base_ref`)
    19â†’  - Phase 2: Provider abstraction + GitHub (optional)
    20â†’
    21â†’## Principles
    22â†’
    23â†’- Reuse, donâ€™t fork: Prefer `internal/arf`, `internal/orchestration`, unified storage/config, existing error envelope.
    24â†’- TDD + phased rollout: Keep phases small with passing unit tests; validate E2E on VPS per CLAUDE.md.
    25â†’- Stable interfaces: New capability should be surfaced behind existing facades where possible.
    26â†’
    27â†’## MVP Additions: LangGraph as Jobs
    28â†’- Planner job: Inputs = repo metadata + last_error + budgets + allowlists + KB snapshot. Output = `plan.json` with parallel options (humanâ€‘step, llmâ€‘exec, ORWâ€‘generated).
    29â†’- Orchestrator fanâ€‘out: Submit each option as its own Nomad job (separate branch); first success wins; cancel others.
    30â†’- Reducer job: Inputs = run history (branch results, winner). Output = next actions (often stop; else new plan).
    31â†’- Learning: Jobs read/write KB in SeaweedFS (cases, summaries) and Consul KV (locks). Error signatures and patch fingerprints dedup fixes.
    32â†’  - See `roadmap/transflow/kb.md` for KB layout, canonicalization, compaction, and promotion rules.
    33â†’
    34â†’## Job Types (Healing Options)
    35â†’- human-step: Pause and wait for human intervention (e.g., push MR or commit); runner polls for branch updates.
    36â†’- llm-exec: Direct LLM patch generation (diff-only) with MCP context/tools; apply and build-check.
    37â†’- orw-gen â†’ openrewrite: Use LLM to generate an OpenRewrite recipe (class/coords) from error context, then run the OpenRewrite job to apply and build-check.
    38â†’
    39â†’Each job type is executed as an isolated Nomad job with explicit allowlists and budgets; LangGraph plans which to launch in parallel and the reducer reconciles results.
    40â†’
    41â†’## Conventions
    42â†’- Branch names: `workflow/<id>/<timestamp>` (workflow) and `exec/<id>/<seq>` (future parallel).
    43â†’- Build app name: `tfw-<id>-<timestamp>`.
    44â†’- Global controls: `lane` (override), `build_timeout` (default 10m) in transflow.yaml.
    45â†’- MR defaults: add labels/scope `ploy`, `tfl` when supported; always include them in description.
    46â†’

     4â†’status: pending|in-progress|completed|blocked
     5â†’created: YYYY-MM-DD
     6â†’modules: [list of services/modules involved]
     7â†’---
     8â†’
     9â†’# [Human-Readable Title]
    10â†’
    11â†’## Problem/Goal
    12â†’[Clear description of what we're solving/building]
    13â†’
    14â†’## Success Criteria
    15â†’- [ ] Specific, measurable outcome
    16â†’- [ ] Another concrete goal
    17â†’
    18â†’## Context Files
    19â†’<!-- Added by context-gathering agent or manually -->
    20â†’- @service/file.py:123-145  # Specific lines
    21â†’- @other/module.py          # Whole file  
    22â†’- patterns/auth-flow        # Pattern reference
    23â†’
    24â†’## User Notes
    25â†’<!-- Any specific notes or requirements from the developer -->
    26â†’
    27â†’## Work Log
    28â†’<!-- Updated as work progresses -->
    29â†’- [YYYY-MM-DD] Started task, initial research

1â†’# Minimal workflow schema (example instance)
     2â†’version: v1alpha1
     3â†’id: my-workflow
     4â†’target_repo: org/project
     5â†’target_branch: refs/heads/main
     6â†’base_ref: refs/heads/main
     7â†’lane: C                 # optional global override (else auto-detect)
     8â†’build_timeout: 10m      # global build timeout
     9â†’self_heal:
    10â†’  max_retries: 2        # retries for ORW-based healing on build failure
    11â†’
    12â†’budgets:
    13â†’  run_max_tokens: 0      # 0 means unlimited
    14â†’  run_max_cost: 0        # 0 means unlimited
    15â†’  run_timeout: 0s        # 0s means unlimited
    16â†’
    17â†’artifacts:
    18â†’  type: mr               # full-archive | diff | mr
    19â†’  target_repo: org/project
    20â†’  storage:
    21â†’    diffs: seaweedfs
    22â†’    process_state: consul_kv
    23â†’
    24â†’mr:
    25â†’  forge: gitlab
    26â†’  repo_url_env: GITLAB_URL
    27â†’  token_env: GITLAB_TOKEN
    28â†’  labels: ["ploy", "tfl"]
    29â†’
    30â†’branch_naming:
    31â†’  workflow_branch: "workflow/${id}/${timestamp}"
    32â†’  exec_branch: "exec/${id}/${seq}"
    33â†’
    34â†’job_defaults:
    35â†’  retries: 3
    36â†’  timeout: 30m
    37â†’
    38â†’network:
    39â†’  default_policy: deny
    40â†’  allow_llm: true
    41â†’  allow_mcp_endpoints: true
    42â†’  additional_allowed_urls: []
    43â†’
    44â†’gates:
    45â†’  enabled: [static_analysis, vuln_scan, compile]
    46â†’  disabled: [deploy, tests]
    47â†’
    48â†’error_policy:
    49â†’  on_failure:
    50â†’    max_retries: 2
    51â†’    fallback: human-step   # halt | human-step
    52â†’
    53â†’merge:
    54â†’  strategy: merge
    55â†’  auto_format: true
    56â†’  recheck_gates_post_merge: true
    57â†’
    58â†’steps:
    59â†’  - type: recipe
    60â†’    id: openrewrite-updates
    61â†’    engine: openrewrite
    62â†’    recipes:
    63â†’      - com.acme.FixNulls
    64â†’      - com.acme.UpdateApi
    65â†’
    66â†’  - type: llm-plan
    67â†’    id: plan-remediations
    68â†’    parallel: true
    69â†’    max_parallel_execs: 10
    70â†’    mcp_tools:
    71â†’      - name: file-system
    72â†’        endpoint: mcp://fs
    73â†’      - name: search
    74â†’        endpoint: mcp://rg
    75â†’    context:
    76â†’      - "src/**/*.java"
    77â†’      - "https://docs.example.com/api/v2"
    78â†’    budgets:
    79â†’      max_tokens: 0
    80â†’      max_cost: 0
    81â†’      timeout: 0s
    82â†’
    83â†’  # Example explicit exec; in practice llm-plan emits exec steps
    84â†’  - type: llm-exec
    85â†’    id: apply-fix-1
    86â†’    model: my-model@v1        # Resolved via ployman model registry (CRUD)
    87â†’    prompts:
    88â†’      - "Refactor null checks to Optional"
    89â†’    mcp_tools:
    90â†’      - name: file-system
    91â†’        endpoint: mcp://fs
    92â†’    context:
    93â†’      - "src/**/*.java"
    94â†’    budgets:
    95â†’      max_tokens: 0
    96â†’      max_cost: 0
    97â†’      timeout: 0s
    98â†’

   100â†’    reason: "Resolve merge conflicts if any"

1â†’package transflow
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"errors"
     5â†’	"fmt"
     6â†’	"io/ioutil"
     7â†’	"strconv"
     8â†’	"time"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"gopkg.in/yaml.v3"
    11â†’)
    12â†’
    13â†’type TransflowStep struct {
    14â†’	Type    string   `yaml:"type"`
    15â†’	ID      string   `yaml:"id"`
    16â†’	Engine  string   `yaml:"engine"`
    17â†’	Recipes []string `yaml:"recipes"`
    18â†’}
    19â†’
    20â†’type TransflowConfig struct {
    21â†’	Version      string          `yaml:"version"`
    22â†’	ID           string          `yaml:"id"`
    23â†’	TargetRepo   string          `yaml:"target_repo"`
    24â†’	TargetBranch string          `yaml:"target_branch"`
    25â†’	BaseRef      string          `yaml:"base_ref"`
    26â†’	Lane         string          `yaml:"lane"`
    27â†’	BuildTimeout string          `yaml:"build_timeout"`
    28â†’	Steps        []TransflowStep `yaml:"steps"`
    29â†’	SelfHeal     *SelfHealConfig `yaml:"self_heal"`
    30â†’}
    31â†’
    32â†’func LoadConfig(path string) (*TransflowConfig, error) {
    33â†’	b, err := ioutil.ReadFile(path)
    34â†’	if err != nil {
    35â†’		return nil, err
    36â†’	}
    37â†’	var cfg TransflowConfig
    38â†’	if err := yaml.Unmarshal(b, &cfg); err != nil {
    39â†’		return nil, err
    40â†’	}
    41â†’
    42â†’	// Set default self-heal config if not provided or apply defaults to existing config
    43â†’	if cfg.SelfHeal == nil {
    44â†’		cfg.SelfHeal = GetDefaultSelfHealConfig()
    45â†’	} else {
    46â†’		// Apply defaults for missing fields
    47â†’		if cfg.SelfHeal.MaxRetries == 0 {
    48â†’			cfg.SelfHeal.MaxRetries = GetDefaultSelfHealConfig().MaxRetries
    49â†’		}
    50â†’		// Cooldown defaults to empty string, so no need to set it

---

status: pending

---

# Complete CLI Integration for Transflow MVP

## Problem/Goal
Complete the end-to-end CLI integration for `ploy transflow run` to make it fully functional per the MVP requirements. While the CLI infrastructure exists, the MVP.md shows "Complete CLI integration (`ploy transflow run`)" as "âťŚ Not Implemented", indicating missing functionality or integration issues that prevent the full transflow workflow from working properly.

The goal is to ensure `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml` executes the complete workflow: recipe execution â†’ build validation â†’ healing (if needed) â†’ GitLab MR creation, with proper error handling and comprehensive testing.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Full end-to-end workflow execution via `ploy transflow run -f transflow.yaml`
- [ ] Integration with existing ARF pipeline for recipe execution
- [ ] Build validation using `SharedPush` to controller `/v1/apps/:app/builds`
- [ ] LangGraph healing integration (planner/reducer jobs) when builds fail
- [ ] GitLab MR creation after successful workflow completion
- [ ] Comprehensive CLI argument parsing and validation
- [ ] Support for all documented CLI flags and options
- [ ] Unit tests for CLI command parsing and workflow orchestration
- [ ] Integration tests demonstrating full workflow functionality
- [ ] Error handling that follows project patterns (graceful failures)
- [ ] Verbose and quiet output modes working correctly
- [ ] Working examples using the provided transflow.yaml template
- [ ] Documentation updates showing CLI usage and examples
- [ ] Mark MVP.md task as completed (âś…)
- [ ] Update CHANGELOG.md with CLI integration completion
- [ ] All tests passing with TDD methodology (RED/GREEN/REFACTOR)

## Context Files
- @roadmap/transflow/MVP.md - Current MVP status and requirements
- @roadmap/transflow/transflow.yaml - Configuration template and examples
- @internal/cli/transflow/run.go - CLI entrypoint and command parsing
- @internal/cli/transflow/runner.go - Core workflow execution engine
- @internal/cli/transflow/config.go - YAML configuration loading and validation
- @cmd/ploy/main.go:47-48 - Main CLI integration point
- @internal/cli/common/deploy.go::SharedPush - Build validation interface
- @internal/orchestration - Job submission and monitoring infrastructure
- @internal/git/provider - GitLab MR integration (recently implemented)

## User Notes
- Follow TDD methodology strictly: RED (failing tests) â†’ GREEN (minimal implementation) â†’ REFACTOR (VPS testing)
- Reuse existing infrastructure where possible (ARF, orchestration, git operations)
- Follow CLI patterns established in other `ploy` commands
- Ensure proper error handling and user feedback for all failure modes
- Test both success and failure scenarios comprehensively
- Validate integration with recently completed GitLab MR functionality
- Use the transflow.yaml template for testing scenarios

## Work Log
- [2025-09-05] Created task following MVP requirements and roadmap documentation
